api: Mixpanel APIs (legacy Query API v2.0 plus current Query and Annotations API references)
scope: This bundle preserves the legacy Mixpanel Query API read contract and adds current list/detail-style GET streams that fit the existing Basic-auth Query/Application API model. It remains read-only: ingestion, identity/profile mutation, schema administration, service-account administration, feature-flag management, experiment management, warehouse connector management, GDPR jobs, raw export downloads, custom JQL, and aggregate report endpoints are documented here as excluded surface because they are mutation/admin, binary/download, custom-query, scalar-array, or aggregate-report APIs rather than finite object-record streams.

excluded.reason: The current Query API documents these read endpoints as form POSTs, but the legacy connector emits the same cohort/profile record families through the older /api/2.0 GET endpoints covered by cohorts and engage; exposing non-mutating POST streams would also trip the conformance write-surface gate for this read-only connector.
These Query API endpoints return aggregate report objects with dynamic date/event/property maps, not stable list/detail object-record collections.
These endpoints return bare scalar string arrays; the declarative stream engine emits object records and would drop scalar array elements.
Custom JQL executes caller-provided JavaScript and accepts application/x-www-form-urlencoded script bodies; it is an ad hoc query executor, not a fixed connector stream or safe write action.
Annotation and tag creation requires Analyst-or-higher project permissions and mutates project metadata; this connector remains read-only.
Annotation patching mutates project metadata and requires elevated project permissions.
Annotation deletion removes project metadata and is intentionally not exposed as reverse ETL.
Raw event export is a bulk download/NDJSON export endpoint on the data export host, not a paginated JSON object-record stream.
Ingestion and identity endpoints create historical/live events or merge identities using project-token/import credentials outside the legacy Query API credential contract.
User and group profile endpoints mutate Mixpanel profiles through the ingestion API and need a separate reverse-ETL design with project-token auth and approval semantics.
Lookup table listing belongs to the ingestion/governance API, not the legacy Query API connector; access requires project-token style credentials outside this bundle's auth hook.
Replacing lookup tables mutates project enrichment data and uses ingestion API credentials outside the legacy Query API auth model.
Lexicon schema reads are administrative data-governance endpoints requiring schema access outside the legacy Query API stream contract.
Lexicon schema create/replace endpoints mutate governance metadata and require admin/owner permissions.
Lexicon schema deletion removes governance metadata and is intentionally not exposed as reverse ETL.
Warehouse Connector API reads expose warehouse import configuration and job history, an administrative surface outside the legacy Query API connector.
Warehouse import creation mutates data pipeline configuration and requires an admin-scoped reverse-ETL design.
Warehouse import updates mutate data pipeline configuration.
Manual warehouse import sync triggers runtime execution of an import job.
Warehouse import deletion can remove import configuration and optionally imported data.
Service-account reads are organization/project administration endpoints and not Mixpanel analytics streams.
Service-account creation and project membership changes mutate organization access control.
Service-account deletion removes an organization credential principal.
GDPR retrieval/deletion endpoints create privacy jobs and require a dedicated compliance workflow.
GDPR job status reads are tied to privacy-job administration, not analytics record sync.
Cancelling a deletion job is a compliance workflow control and not a connector data operation.
Feature flag evaluation/management uses separate feature-flag API hosts and rollout permissions outside the legacy Query API credential contract.
Feature flag creation mutates rollout configuration.
Feature flag updates mutate rollout configuration.
Feature flag deletion removes rollout configuration.
Experiments are product-management configuration objects outside the legacy Query API analytics stream contract.
Experiment creation/archive actions mutate experiment lifecycle state.
Experiment updates and decisions mutate experiment configuration or state.
Experiment launch/conclusion changes live experiment lifecycle state.
Experiment deletion/unarchive lifecycle operations are administrative controls outside reverse ETL.
Data Pipelines reads expose export-pipeline configuration/status on the data export host rather than analytics records.
Data Pipeline create/edit/cancel/pause/resume endpoints mutate export pipeline configuration or execution state.
